content:
Here is how you can insert comments in Java:

```java
/**
 * Class C.
 */
class C {

    /**
     * Method foo.
     *
     * @param i The first parameter.
     * @param array The rest of the parameters.
     * @return int
     */
    int foo(int i, int... array) {
        return 42;
    }
}
```

In this code:

- `/**` starts a block of comments.
- `Class C.` indicates that this is a class named "C".
- `int foo(int i, int... array)` indicates the method named "foo". It takes two parameters: an integer (`i`) and an arbitrary number of integers (`array`).
- `@return int` indicates that the return type of the method is an integer.
- The rest of the code within `{}` is a block of comments. Here, it's explaining what the method does.
- `*/` ends the comment block.

```java
This program multiplies two variables: height and width, then assigns the result to a new variable temp.

*/

public class Main {
    public static void main(String[] args) {
        int height = 5; // Define height
        int width = 10; // Define width

        int temp = 2 * (height + width); // Multiply height and width, store the result in temp
        System.out.println(temp); // Print out the value of temp

        temp = height * width; // Multiply height and width again, store the result in temp
        System.out.println(temp); // Print out the value of temp
    }
}
```
